input.headline: How to turn hazelnuts into a brilliant flour for cakes – recipe

raw.headline: How to turn hazelnuts into a brilliant flour for cakes – recipe

winner: My wife tried making nutty cake from scratch. We ended up with a room that smelled like a squirrel’s gym and a kitchen floor covered in "hazel-flour" – which was just ground-up hope and a few actual nuts. She called it *The Great Crumble*.

candidates.text: Why did the cake baker get stuck in the nut mill? Because he heard making hazelnut flour was *nutty* enough, but turning it into a brilliant idea was *crushed*!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative wordplay on 'nutty' and 'crushed,' ties well with the headline, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Why did the hazelnut refuse to bake in my cake? It said, "I'd rather be a *nut* than a *nut*-tier!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'nut' and 'nut-tier' are clever and specific to the topic, with a surprising twist that fits the headline well."}

candidates.text: My wife tried making nutty cake from scratch. We ended up with a room that smelled like a squirrel’s gym and a kitchen floor covered in "hazel-flour" – which was just ground-up hope and a few actual nuts. She called it *The Great Crumble*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative and specific imagery with a clever twist on 'hazel-flour' and the title of the disaster cake."}

candidates.text: I tried to make a cake from scratch using that "brilliant" hazelnut flour—next time I'll stick to the boxed kind... at least then if it flops, I can blame the instructions and not my own incompetence.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is relatable and has a bit of self-deprecating humor, but the surprise and specificity are weak. It's somewhat generic and doesn't fully engage with the headline's focus on the flour's brilliance."}

candidates.text: Crack open some nuts, mix them with a splash of boredom, and you've got yourself a cake that won't even notice if you forget the sugar.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the 'splash of boredom' twist, but it's a bit vague and doesn't fully tie into the recipe aspect. The humor is light but lacks the specificity and surprise that would make it stand out in a pairwise comparison."}
